attributes.action: To abolish the modern-day slavery practise in the UK
attributes.additional_details: We call on the UK Government to strengthen laws against technology-enabled harassment, unlawful surveillance, coercive control, cyberstalking, and organised intimidation. No individual should have their freedom, privacy, well-being, or daily life disrupted through the misuse of technology or coordinated abuse without accountability. Stronger protections, investigations, support, and oversight are needed to prevent modern forms of exploitation and psychological harm in the UK.
attributes.background: To abolish the practise of modern-day slavery that takes away the freedom of an individual in the UK. The abuse of technology to illegally monitor, disrupt, and cause havoc in the lives of individuals. Using the community of these individuals to make their lives difficult without consequences.

rejection.details: I'm afraid we don't find you petition clear enough for it to proceed. On many of the general points you raise, there has been action recently by Governments - or action is in progress. There isn't anything precise enough in your wording that would allow someone to know exactly what it was that you want to see changed - and in what way.
